title: monardella nana subsp. tenuiflora
intro: Monardella nana subsp. tenuiflora, also known as dwarf mountain monardella, is a low-growing, shrubby perennial plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of using Monardella nana subsp. tenuiflora include reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and protecting against oxidative stress. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as teas and infusions, can be made by steeping the leaves and flowers in hot water, and it can also be used in salves and ointments for topical applications.
